target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, and Paul Sabarny. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage.

The peer-median method, applied across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yields a median valuation of $150M, with an observed range of $110M to $200M. This represents the primary triangulation input given the available data. The company has raised €140M+ to date, comprising a €55M Series A round, a €26.1M NRW grant, and a €63.4M BMWE grant.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M (low) to $172M (high), centered on $150M. Key uncertainties include the limited single-method triangulation base and reliance on only three peer comparables to establish market positioning. The substantial grant funding (€89.5M) relative to equity raised suggests either below-market risk profile or sector-specific government support dynamics that may not be fully reflected in traditional peer benchmarking. Additional revenue, unit economics, or technology differentiation data would strengthen conviction in this range.
